title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak with melted garlic butter and fresh herbs on a cutting board.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak, perfectly seared and basted with rich garlic butter.

description: Savor the ultimate New York Strip Steak, expertly seared to a beautiful crust and basted with a fragrant garlic herb butter. This classic American dinner is incredibly tender, bursting with rich savory flavors, and perfect for a special occasion or an elevated weeknight meal.

ingredients.notes: or other high smoke point oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York Strip steaks from the refrigerator about 30-45 minutes before cooking to allow them to come to room temperature. Pat them completely dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Heat Skillet for Searing
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat until it begins to smoke slightly. Add the olive oil to the hot pan and swirl to coat the bottom.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. Use tongs to sear the edges for about 1 minute each.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Herbs
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cold unsalted butter, minced garlic, fresh rosemary sprigs, and fresh thyme sprigs to the skillet. The butter should melt and foam. Tilt the pan slightly to pool the butter and use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the aromatic butter for 2-3 minutes.

instructions.title: Continue Cooking to Desired Doneness
instructions.text: Continue to baste the steaks, flipping them occasionally, until they reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 130-135°F (54-57°C); for medium, 135-140°F (57-60°C). Use an instant-read thermometer for accuracy.

instructions.title: Rest the Steaks
instructions.text: Once the steaks reach your desired doneness, remove them from the skillet and place them on a cutting board. Loosely tent them with aluminum foil and let them rest for at least 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist steak.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: After resting, slice the New York Strip steaks against the grain into thick pieces. Spoon any remaining garlic butter sauce from the pan over the sliced steak before serving immediately. Enjoy with your favorite American side dishes.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always pat your steaks thoroughly dry before seasoning and searing. This ensures a beautiful, crispy crust, which is a hallmark of perfectly cooked American steakhouse-style beef.

For the best flavor, use high-quality, fresh ingredients for your garlic butter. Fresh rosemary and thyme make a significant difference compared to dried herbs. If you don't have fresh, use half the amount of dried herbs.

Serve your New York Strip Steak immediately after resting with the pan juices spooned over. Pair it with classic American sides like mashed potatoes, creamed spinach, or a crisp green salad for a complete meal.
article: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a beloved American Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
